The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA), enacted in July 2025, makes domestic content requirements mandatory for qualifying for the Production Tax Credits (45Y) as part of its major revisions to clean energy incentives. The law harmonized the domestic content requirements under Section 45Y for projects starting construction on or after June 16, 2025, and outlines specific percentages for meeting these mandates. These changes confirm the domestic content requirements becoming a mandatory criterion for receiving 45Y credits in 2025.

The H.R.1 (One Big Beautiful Bill Act) signed into law in July 2025 amends the IRA tax credits to make domestic content requirements mandatory for obtaining the Investment Tax Credit (ITC) under section 48E, with revised thresholds depending on the start of construction date. These requirements are clearly outlined in the White & Case LLP report summarizing the law.

However, it does not specify new domestic materials or subcomponents requirements. If enacted, the AMPTC will have some restrictions, but not material/subcomponent domestic content mandates.
